comment: Clean Code is blunt in a good way. The naming and function-size chapters were the most useful for me.
The Pragmatic Programmer feels older in spots, but the advice on habits and trade-offs still lands.
Python Crash Course was actually beginner-friendly. I got through the basics fast without feeling lost.
Automate the Boring Stuff is super practical. The file and spreadsheet examples were the parts I reused right away.
Grokking Algorithms explains the ideas without trying to sound smarter than the reader. Good intro book.
Introduction to Algorithms is useful, but definitely not a casual read. More reference book than bedtime story.
Head First Design Patterns made the examples easier to remember. Way less dry than I expected.
Effective Python is nice when you already know the basics and want sharper habits. Short chapters, low nonsense.
Cracking the Coding Interview is still solid for practice. The walkthroughs made the harder questions less annoying.
You Don't Know JS Yet goes deeper than most JS books. Not beginner-light, but great once the basics already click.
